rule: Ammo counting should be ignored when the GM disables ammo tracking.
currentEvidence: trackBlasterCharges setting exists in houserule-settings/manifest/presets, but AmmoSystem, roll-config, and inventory ammo displays do not appear to query it before enforcing/displaying ammo.

rule: Autofire requires 10 shots; Burst Fire requires 5 shots; attack options should grey out when ammo is insufficient and ammo tracking is enabled.
currentEvidence: roll-config enables Autofire by weapon capability and Burst Fire by capability + feat, with no current/max ammo gate, reload control, or not-enough-shots warning.

rule: When ammo tracking is off, ammo UI should disappear completely. When on, Combat, Gear, and attack dialog should share ammo pills/reload controls.
currentEvidence: Old v2 inventory templates show raw current/max ammo unconditionally when system.ammunition exists. Concept weapon cards do not show ammo UI. No shared ammo presenter found.

rule: Reload should spend proper action economy before refilling weapon ammo.
currentEvidence: AmmoSystem.reloadWeapon can refill current ammo to max, but no reload button/action handler or action-economy spend was found.
automationBoundary: automate action cost; GM-managed ammo source until inventory source authority exists

rule: Burst Fire is a single-target attack and does not deal half damage on a miss.
currentEvidence: enhanced-rolls rollAutofire Burst Fire branch sets damageType half and halves final damage on miss.
automationBoundary: automate single-target Burst Fire; no area miss damage

rule: Burst Fire should modify a single ranged attack; action metadata should not conflict.
currentEvidence: data/combat-actions.json lists Burst Fire as standard action, while data/feat-combat-actions.json lists it as full-round action.

rule: Weapon Autofire capability should come from canonical weapon data and should not treat stripped autofire as present.
currentEvidence: roll-config and CombatOptionResolver check system.autofire/properties, but enhanced-rolls checks properties includes autofire OR strippedFeatures.autofire === true and misses system.autofire.

rule: Evasion belongs to each target affected by an area attack, not the attacker.
currentEvidence: enhanced-rolls rollAutofire checks actor.items for Evasion while iterating targetResults.
automationBoundary: automate target talent lookup once damage context is canonical

rule: Weapons with stun setting should expose Lethal/Stun mode and preserve stun context into damage.
currentEvidence: Damage type stun exists, Switch Weapon Mode action exists, and stun weapons exist in data, but no Lethal/Stun selector or current weapon mode pipeline was found in the attack dialog.
automationBoundary: automate mode/context; apply damage effects carefully

rule: Ammo shot pills should use selected blaster bolt color for flair.
currentEvidence: WeaponVisualProfileResolver already resolves boltColor from flags/system/defaults; weapon edit UI exposes flags.swse.boltColor.
